Arboriculture is the selection, production and care of plants used in landscape architecture.

Aquaculture is the farming of aquatic organisms such as crustaceans, molluscs, algae and aquatic plants.
Prokaryotic cells contain membrane-enclosed organelles, including a DNA in nucleus.

Darwin proposed artificial selection as the mechanism for evolutionary adaptation of population to their environments.
Energy flows through the ecosystem.

Natural selection is the evolutionary process.

At each level of biological hierarchy, a correlation of form and structure can be observed.
While hovering, the hummingbirds can extend their short, slender beaks into flowers and feed on nectar.

Consumers convert energy from sunlight to chemical energy.
Some of the chemical energy is lost from the ecosystem as water.

Interactions among plants, animals, and other organisms affect the participants in same ways.

Biologists classify species according to a system of broader and broader groups.

Agriculture is defined to produce commodities which maintain life.
The commodities production of agriculture include thee foods, fibers, forest products, horticultural crops and their related services.
The global major agricultural products can be grouped into foods, fibers, fuels and raw materials.
In Myanmar, most farm producing rice paddy during the dry seasons.
Commercially cultivating agricultural crops in Myanmar are mainly cereals, pulses and onions crops.
There are two dominant rice production systems; rain fed lowland and irrigated upland.
The traditional method for cultivating rice is flooding the fields while or after, setting the young seedlings.
Wheat is cereal grain that is a worldwide staple food.
The wheat can be cultivated twice a year.
The zero-tillage technique is a traditional method of wheat cultivation.
Maize is a tropical plant that prefers warm humid weather.
Zea mays is a short-day plant.
Maize seeds should not be sown more than 5-6 cm depth of soil.
Among the ASEAN countries, Myanmar is the leading paddy and maize producer.
Myanmar is the second-largest producer of pulses after India.
About 8 types of pulses are produced in Myanmar.
Horticulture involves plant propagation and cultivation to improve the plant.
The word agriculture is derives from latin words “agri” and “cultura”.
Horticulture is the Latin words, 'hortus' mean 'garden' and 'cultura' mean 'cultivation'.
Agriculture does include the intensive crop farming and large-scale crop production.
Horticulture focuses on one large primary crop at a time.
Olericulture is dealing with the culture of non-woody plants for foods.
Fruiticulture is the production of grapes.
Viticulture is largely intended for wine making.
Floriculture is the growing and marketing of flowers and ornamental plants for floristry.
Turf management is the production and upkeep of turf, artificial and live, for use in recreation.
Landscape horticulture is the cultivation and care of individual trees, shrubs, vines and woody plants.
Postharvest physiology is the management of harvested horticultural crops.
Hydroponic is a way of growing in soil and water.
Hydroponics is a method of growing plants without soil and can't grow hydroponically all year long.
Hydroponics uses more water than traditional soil-based systems.
Hydroponics is a type of horticulture and a subset of hydroculture.
Terrestrial or aquatic plants may grow with their roots exposed to the nutritious liquid.
The nutrients used in hydroponic systems can come from many different sources.
Plants are commonly grown hydroponically in greenhouse, on inert media.
Hydroponics offers many advantages, notably an increase in water usage in agriculture.
Hydroponics takes much less water to grow plants.
Aquafarming, also known as aquaculture.
Aquaculture involves cultivating freshwater, brackish water and saltwater populations.
Aquaculture is the harvesting of wild fish.
Aquaculture system is similar to commercial fishing.
Commercial fishing is the harvesting of wild fish.
Pisciculture involves commercial breeding of fish.
The most important fish species used in fish farming are, in order, carp, salmon, tilapia and catfish.
Mariculture, also known as pisciculture.
